page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and database operations. The instance achieves strong ratings in large-block uncached memory read (218.61 GB/sec) and write (95.85 GB/sec) operations, alongside a strong PassMark database operations score of 21602.37. Multi-core performance is average, with a PassMark CPU score of 44173.28 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 12132.0. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by the stress-ng single-core score of 1327.68 and weak single-threaded compression metrics.

From a resource density perspective, the m5dn.16xlarge offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory alongside high-speed local NVMe storage and high network bandwidth. This profile makes it highly suitable for data-intensive workloads, database hosting, and distributed file systems that require high sequential memory throughput and fast local storage. However, the lower single-core performance suggests it is less optimal for single-threaded processing tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose, Nitro-based instance designed for network and storage-intensive workloads. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations, while single-core compute performance remains relatively weak. This hardware combination makes the instance well-suited for database hosting, data-intensive applications, and workloads requiring high network throughput and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, 2400 GB NVMe SSD, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A general-purpose platform featuring high-speed local NVMe storage, robust memory bandwidth, and high-capacity network throughput for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 3.1 GHz processor
256 GB of DDR4 memory and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations
Suited for database hosting, data-intensive applications, and network-heavy workloads
